summary: Full-stack e2e test of GurucraftPro MVP. 31/31 pytest backend tests passed (auth, services, products, testimonials, gallery, learning, templates, coupons, orders+payments mock flow, contact, designs, admin stats & CRUD, role-based access). Frontend smoke + integration: home renders (hero, 6 services, 4 testimonials, navbar, footer), admin login redirects to /admin and shows 11 tabs + correct stats, signup -> shop -> add-to-cart -> /checkout -> mock pay -> order shows as 'paid' on dashboard (₹449.1 indicates 10% coupon applied to ₹499 product, but coupon was NOT explicitly applied in test - investigate). Design Studio loads: add-text/rect/circle/triangle, undo/redo, save, PNG/JPG/PDF export buttons, 4 templates, layers panel, color picker. Contact form submits without console errors.

minor.issue: FastAPI on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated. Migrate to lifespan context manager for forward compatibility.
OrderCreate accepts arbitrary item.price from client — server should look up real prices from DB to prevent client-side price tampering.

Endpoint is unauthenticated and idempotently marks any order as 'paid'. With Razorpay keys present this is fine (signature checked); but in mock mode any caller can mark any order paid. Acceptable for demo MVP per agent_to_agent_context_note but worth noting.
Status passed as query string (status_val) instead of JSON body — inconsistent with other endpoints.

integration_issues.issue: After successful POST /api/contact, no visible success toast/message detected in DOM body text. Backend stored the message (verified via admin endpoint test). Likely a toast that auto-dismisses, but worth confirming a 'Thanks, we will reach out' confirmation persists.

action_items: (Optional) Migrate FastAPI startup/shutdown handlers to lifespan API.
(Recommended) Verify item prices server-side in POST /api/orders by joining with services/products/learning collections instead of trusting client payload.
(Optional) Display a persistent toast/inline success message after Contact form submission for better UX.

Add data-testid attributes to Login/Signup form inputs and the Shop add-to-cart, cart-open, checkout-coupon-input fields to make future UI automation more robust (currently relying on placeholder/text selectors).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 884 lines — consider splitting into routers/ (auth, services, products, orders, admin, payments) for maintainability.
No Pydantic email-validator listed in requirements.txt explicitly — works because installed transitively. Pin it.
JWT_SECRET default fallback 'changeme' in code is a security smell; require it explicitly via os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] to fail fast.
Mongo _id correctly excluded with {'_id': 0} projection on every find — verified across all responses.
Order price is computed from client-supplied items[].price — server should authoritatively price-check against DB.
/api/payments/verify lacks auth and signature check is bypassed in mock mode — fine for demo, but document clearly.
test_credentials: admin@gurucraftpro.in / Gurucraftpro (seeded). Test users created dynamically by tests with prefix test_/shopper_.
seed_data_creation: All seed data (6 services, 6 products, 4 testimonials, 7 gallery items, 4 learning items, 4 templates, 2 coupons WELCOME10/GURUJI25, admin user) is created automatically by server.py @app.on_event('startup'). Confirmed present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all 31 critical paths. Razorpay is intentionally in mock mode (RAZORPAY_KEY_ID/SECRET empty) — /api/payments/create-order returns mock razorpay_order_id, /api/payments/verify marks order paid without signature check. Admin email admin@gurucraftpro.in / Gurucraftpro. Frontend uses placeholder-based selectors for signup/login (no data-testid on form inputs); studio and admin tabs have data-testid; checkout pay button is data-testid='checkout-pay'.
